Dewatering: Dewatering to allow for high-temp roasting
Sulfate Roasting: Roasting of lepidolate with sulfate salts

Description: Key equation, chemical or physical, evaluated in this step.
Impurity Removal and Neutralization: Impurity precipitation / neutralization, e.g. Fe³⁺ + 3OH⁻ → Fe(OH)₃(s); Mg²⁺ + CO₃²⁻ → MgCO₃(s); Ca²⁺ + CO₃²⁻ → CaCO₃(s); Al³⁺ + 3OH⁻ → Al(OH)₃(s). Lithium remains mostly in solution.

Crushing: Primary & secondary crushing, generates crushed ore for wet circuit
Classification & Flotation: Hydrocyclones+screens to split coarse vs fine; assume coarse refeed to achieve input/output; fines feed thickener
Dewatering: Go from 40% water slurry to 20% water cake. 60% slurry has density of 1/(0.60/2.7+0.4/1) = 1.607 --> 0.6*1.607 = 0.965 t/m3 solids. At 80% cake, new density is 0.964/0.8 = 1.205 t/m3; water goes from 0.643 t/m3 --> 0.241 t/m3, or ~0.4 t/m3≈0.4 m3/m3 water removed from intermediate slurry
Impurity Removal and Neutralization: Chemical removal of impurities (Mg, Ca, Fe, Al, etc.) via pH adjustment and precipitation; produces purified Li solution for downstream recovery and an impurity sludge for disposal.

Description: Name of the intermediate volume inputs are aggregated into and outputs produced from

Dewatering: m^3 slurry being pressed into cake
Sulfate Roasting: tonnes (80% solids cake being dried, roasted, and cooled)
Clarification: m^3 pregant leach solution being clarified
